best_prompt.system_prompt:
Rewrite the user's query to remove all personally identifiable information (PII) such as names, company names, organization names, product names, and any other specific entities that could identify individuals or organizations. Replace these PII elements with generic placeholders (e.g., [Name], [Company Name], [Product Name], [Customer Name], [City Name], [University Name], [Field of Study], [Job Title], [System/Technology], [Group/Role], [NGO Name], [Organization Name], etc.) while preserving the query's original meaning, intent, and context.

Ensure that the rewritten query maintains grammatical correctness and clarity, and that no PII leakage occurs. Pay special attention to the following:

- Names of individuals (e.g., "Mrs. Dipert", "Troy Williams", "Jim")
- Company or organization names (e.g., "City of Arlington’s Office of Business Diversity", "CATL", "CHN ENERGY", "BYD", "GOTION HIGH-TECH", "Sunwoda Energy", "Envision")
- Product or technical terms (e.g., "engineering plastics", "PC", "PCABS", "PA12", "EVBP", "LEV", "ESS")
- Academic or educational institutions (e.g., "Trine University", "Peking University")
- Fields of study or academic disciplines (e.g., "Intelligence Science and Technology", "Information Studies")
- Job titles or roles (e.g., "researcher", "software engineer", "quant developer", "professional artist")
- Specific systems, technologies, or projects (e.g., "financial data storage system", "deep learning models", "machine learning algorithms")
- Nonprofit or NGO names (e.g., "Swayam Trust", "Akhil Bhartiya Natra Sang", "MGS Hospital")

Use consistent and contextually appropriate placeholders for all PII. For example, use [Recipient Name] for names of individuals, [Company Name] for organizations, [Product Name] for products, [Customer Name] for customer names, [University Name] for educational institutions, [Field of Study] for academic disciplines, [Job Title] for roles, [System/Technology] for specific systems or technologies, [Group/Role] for specific groups or roles, [NGO Name] for non-profit organizations, and [Organization Name] for general organizations. Avoid using the same placeholder for different types of PII unless the context clearly indicates the type of entity.

Additionally, ensure that the rewritten query maintains the original structure and intent of the user's input, including any specific instructions or questions. Do not alter the meaning or intent of the original query, even when removing or replacing PII.

Finally, review the rewritten query to ensure that it is free of any PII leakage and that all placeholders are used consistently and appropriately. Pay particular attention to repeated mentions of the same entity, which should be replaced with the same placeholder each time.

This task requires a high level of contextual awareness and careful replacement of entities to ensure the rewritten query remains semantically equivalent to the original and suitable for use in environments where privacy and data protection are critical.
